subtitle: A foreign monk walks the shining roads of Gupta India and sees both gold and shadow

panels.shot: wide, low-angle view of Faxian standing at the edge of a great Indian city with distant stupas and palace towers glowing in dusk light
panels.action: Faxian the Pilgrim stands with travel staff and scroll case on a dusty road, gazing toward a sprawling Gupta-era city whose walls, gateways, and distant stupas rise over the trees in the warm light of sunset.

dialogue.text: In the age of Gupta kings, a lone monk from China walks into India, seeking sacred words and finding a golden land whose splendour and scars he quietly records.

i18n.hi: गुप्त राजाओं के युग में, चीन से आया अकेला भिक्षु भारत में प्रवेश करता है, धर्म की खोज में, और एक स्वर्ण देश देखता है जिसकी चमक और चोटें वह चुपचाप लिखता जाता है।

panels.shot: medium shot, side view, desert road fading into distant mountains
panels.action: Faxian trudges along a barren, rocky path, robes dusty, clutching his staff and a small bundle, with distant mountains marking the horizon under a harsh sun.

dialogue.text: For ten long years, Faxian has left behind the valleys of China, crossing deserts and passes, walking west and south in search of the Buddha’s words lost in his homeland.

i18n.hi: दस लंबे वर्षों से फाशियान चीन की घाटियाँ छोड़, रेगिस्तान और दर्रे पार कर, पश्चिम–दक्षिण की ओर चल पड़ा है, बुद्ध के वे वचन खोजने जो उसके देश में खो गए हैं।

panels.shot: bird's-eye wide shot of a caravan trail descending from a pass into greener plains
panels.action: From high above, Faxian is a small figure among traders and pack animals as a caravan winds down from a rocky pass into a lush green plain dotted with villages and smoke trails.

dialogue.text: At last the land softens. Beyond the stony heights of the northwest, the plains of India open, rich and green, threaded with rivers, villages, and roads alive with merchants and monks.

i18n.hi: आख़िर भूमि मुलायम होती है। उत्तर–पश्चिम की पत्थरीली ऊँचाइयों के पार, भारत के समृद्ध हरे मैदान खुलते हैं, नदियों, गाँवों और व्यापारियों–भिक्षुओं से भरी राहों के साथ।

panels.shot: slow push-in on Faxian’s face as he watches a busy highway
panels.action: Standing by a broad, packed earth road, Faxian watches ox-carts, horsemen, and barefoot travelers pass in both directions, his face lit with thoughtful curiosity.

dialogue.text: He enters a realm held together by roads. Royal highways bind city to city, ferrying grain, soldiers, pilgrims, and gold coins stamped with kings whose names he does not yet know.

i18n.hi: वह ऐसे राज्य में प्रवेश करता है जो सड़कों से बँधा है। राज–मार्ग नगरों को जोड़ते हैं, अनाज, सैनिक, यात्री और सुनहरी मुद्राएँ ढोते हुए, उन राजाओं के नाम लिए जो उसे अभी अनजान हैं।

panels.shot: wide shot of city gate with guards and bustle
panels.action: Faxian approaches a fortified city gate where neatly dressed guards stand alert as townsfolk, craftsmen with tools, and traders with laden carts move through in orderly streams.

dialogue.text: From his bamboo records we hear his wonder: cities well-governed, streets clean, laws enforced, no chaos at the gates. In this kingdom, he writes, the people are not harried by harsh imposts.

i18n.hi: उसकी बाँस की पोटली से उसकी हैरानी झाँकती है: सुशासित नगर, साफ़ सड़कें, क़ानून सख़्ती से लागू, फाटक पर कोई अराजकता नहीं। वह लिखता है, यहाँ प्रजा पर ज़ुल्मी करों की मार नहीं।

panels.shot: medium interior shot in a merchant house
panels.action: Inside a prosperous merchant’s courtyard, Faxian sits respectfully on a low bench as a well-dressed trader weighs gleaming gold coins on a small balance in front of sacks of goods.

dialogue.text: He sees gold dinaras in merchant hands, bright discs of the Gupta mint, and notes how trade has fattened the markets. To him, it seems a land without want and without forced levies.

i18n.hi: वह व्यापारियों के हाथों में सुनहरे दीनार देखता है, गुप्त टकसाल की चमकती मुद्राएँ, और नोट करता है कि व्यापार ने बाज़ारों को तृप्त कर दिया है। उसे यह भूमि कमी और जबरन वसूली से मुक्त दिखती है।

panels.shot: low-angle wide of royal land grant ceremony under a tree, viewed from behind villagers
panels.action: From behind a group of bare-bodied cultivators, we see a royal officer seated under a tree, handing a copper plate to a serene Brahmin while scribes and attendants look on.

dialogue.text: Yet beyond his gaze, royal grants of village and field flow to Brahmins and officers. Copper plates carve out agraharas, where old village rights and dues now bend toward new lords.

i18n.hi: पर उसकी नज़र से परे, गाँव और खेतों के शाही दान ब्राह्मणों और अधिकारियों को बहते हैं। ताम्रपट पर उकेरे जाते हैं अग्रहार, जहाँ पुराने गाँव–अधिकार अब नए स्वामियों की ओर झुकते हैं।

panels.shot: medium shot of peasants in the background being directed by an overseer, Faxian passing on the road nearby
panels.action: As Faxian walks along a country road, in the fields beyond men and women bend over earth while a stern overseer with a staff gestures, assigning tasks to weary labourers.

dialogue.text: R.S. Sharma and later scholars read behind the glitter: labourers bound by vishti, compelled service; villages pledged to grantees, peasants carrying the weight beneath the royal shine.

i18n.hi: आर.एस. शर्मा और आगे के विद्वान चमक के पीछे को पढ़ते हैं: विश्टि से बँधे मज़दूर, मजबूर सेवा; दान में बँधे गाँव, राज–चमक के नीचे बोझ ढोते किसान।

panels.shot: wide shot of a monastery complex on a hilltop overlooking a river
panels.action: A serene monastery crowns a low hill above a broad river, monks moving silently between halls, while Faxian climbs the stone steps with scrolls clutched to his chest.

dialogue.text: Across this golden realm, monasteries thrive. Gupta kings and guilds endow vihāras; monks study, debate, and copy. For Faxian, each cloister is a haven, each library a long-sought treasure.

i18n.hi: इस स्वर्ण realm भर में विहार फलते हैं। गुप्त राजा और श्रेणियाँ विहारों को दान देते हैं; भिक्षु पढ़ते, वाद करते, प्रतिलिपि करते हैं। फाशियान के लिए हर मठ शरण है, हर पुस्तकालय मिला–जुला ख़ज़ाना।

panels.shot: interior medium shot in a monastery library
panels.action: In a dim lamp-lit hall lined with palm-leaf bundles, Faxian kneels beside a low desk, carefully unrolling a manuscript as an elder Indian monk gestures, explaining a passage.

dialogue.speaker: faxian_the_pilgrim
dialogue.text: At last, the Vinaya as the Buddha taught it…

i18n.hi: आख़िर वही विनय, जैसा बुद्ध ने बताया…

panels.shot: wide establishing shot of Pataliputra with river, walls, and palaces
panels.action: A sweeping view reveals Pataliputra on the river’s edge, with long timber palisades, tiled roofs, towers, and a broad riverfront busy with boats and steps leading to the water.

dialogue.text: In time he reaches Pataliputra, once the city of Ashoka, now a capital of Gupta power. Here, he will witness what he calls a land where mercy for the poor is a public duty.

i18n.hi: चलते–चलते वह पाटलिपुत्र पहुँचता है, कभी अशोक की नगरी, अब गुप्त शक्ति की राजधानी। यहीं वह उस देश को देखेगा जिसे वह गरीबों के प्रति दया का सार्वजनिक कर्तव्य कहता है।

panels.shot: medium street-level shot in Pataliputra
panels.action: From street level, Faxian walks through a bustling but orderly bazaar, past tidy stalls and sweeping shopkeepers, while a city watchman quietly observes from a corner.

dialogue.text: He notes the city’s order. Streets swept, markets regulated, punishments measured, not wild. In his record, he marvels that the law does not crush people with petty exactions.

i18n.hi: वह नगर की अनुशासन देखता है। झाड़ी हुई सड़कें, नियंत्रित बाज़ार, सज़ाएँ नाप–तौल कर, न बेख़ौफ़। अपने लेख में वह चकित है कि क़ानून लोग को छोटे–छोटे करों से नहीं पीसता।

panels.shot: medium interior shot of a charitable hospital hall
panels.action: Inside a simple yet clean hall, attendants move between rows of low beds where the sick and frail rest; Faxian stands at the entrance, watching as a nurse offers a bowl and medicine to an old man.

dialogue.text: Here he finds what few of his world could imagine: charitable hospitals. He writes of houses where the poor, destitute, crippled and diseased may come, receive food and medicine without payment.

i18n.hi: यहाँ वह वह चीज़ देखता है जिसका उसके संसार में कम ही ख़याल था: दान–अस्पताल। वह उन घरों का वर्णन करता है जहाँ गरीब, निराश्रित, अपाहिज और रोगी आकर बिना मूल्य भोजन और औषधि पाते हैं।

panels.shot: close-up of Faxian’s face as he watches a doctor examine a patient
panels.action: In close view, Faxian’s eyes soften as he looks toward a physician gently examining a patient’s hand, his own lips parted in quiet astonishment.

dialogue.speaker: faxian_the_pilgrim
dialogue.text: A kingdom is judged by the care it gives the helpless.

i18n.hi: राज्य उसी से आँका जाता है कि वह असहायों की कैसी देखभाल करता है।

panels.shot: medium interior shot of a wealthy household with veiled women in an upper balcony
panels.action: From the courtyard below, Faxian sits with a host while above, women watch from behind latticed screens, their figures half-hidden by veils as servants move about.

dialogue.text: But other currents run under the calm surface. Caste coils tighter around birth; women’s worlds shrink behind screens. Later historians will note how new laws narrow their rights and freedom.

i18n.hi: पर सतह की इस शांति के नीचे और धाराएँ बहती हैं। जाति जन्म पर और कसी जाती है; स्त्रियों की दुनिया परदे के भीतर सिमटती जाती है। आगे के इतिहासकार लिखेंगे कि नए क़ानून उनके अधिकार और आज़ादी घटाते हैं।

panels.shot: wide shot of a village scene where a Brahmin teacher instructs upper-caste boys while labourers work nearby
panels.action: In a village clearing, a Brahmin teacher sits under a tree teaching a group of boys with palm-leaf texts, while at the edge of the scene, darker-skinned labourers carry water and wood, excluded from the circle.

dialogue.text: Learning flourishes, but not for all. Sanskrit schools bloom for the few, while lower orders and untouchables bear fields and streets on their shoulders, their stories barely entering any record.

i18n.hi: विद्या खिलती है, पर सबके लिए नहीं। संस्कृत पाठशालाएँ चुनिंदा के लिए हैं, जबकि निचली जातियाँ और अछूत कंधों पर खेत और सड़कें उठाए हैं, उनकी कथाएँ मुश्किल से किसी अभिलेख में पहुँचती हैं।

panels.shot: medium shot of Faxian writing by lamplight in a modest lodging
panels.action: By a small oil lamp in a modest room, Faxian sits cross-legged, brush in hand, carefully writing on a strip as his travel bundle and staff rest nearby.

dialogue.text: In the quiet of his lodging, he gathers his days into ink. He records the monasteries, the discipline, the charity, the coins, the laws, always with the eyes of a monk seeking the Dharma.

i18n.hi: अपने निवास की शांति में वह अपने दिनों को स्याही में बाँधता है। वह विहारों, अनुशासन, दान, मुद्राएँ, क़ानून सब दर्ज करता है, हमेशा उस भिक्षु की नज़र से जो धर्म की खोज में है।

panels.shot: wide shot of a ship on the sea with storm clouds, Faxian visible on deck
panels.action: A broad wooden ship rides heavy swells beneath looming clouds; on deck, Faxian clings to a mast, robes whipped by wind, guarding a bamboo chest lashed with ropes.

dialogue.text: At last, bearing scriptures and memories, he turns seaward. Storms lash him on the ocean road, yet he clings to his chest of texts, to carry India’s words back to distant Luoyang.

i18n.hi: आख़िर, ग्रंथ और स्मृतियाँ सँभाले, वह सागर की ओर मुड़ता है। समुद्री राह पर आँधियाँ उसे झकझोरती हैं, फिर भी वह अपने ग्रंथ–संदूक से चिपका रहता है, ताकि भारत के वचन दूरस्थ लुओयांग पहुँचा सके।

panels.shot: medium shot in a Chinese hall where Faxian reads to a small group of monks
panels.action: In a simple hall with Chinese rafters, Faxian sits before a small assembly of monks, unrolling a manuscript as they listen intently, scrolls and brushes laid before them.

dialogue.text: In China his Record of Buddhist Kingdoms takes shape. Through his careful lines, later ages glimpse Gupta India: its hospitals and highways, its gilded courts and silent labourers, its light and its shadow.

i18n.hi: चीन में उसका ‘बुद्ध–राज्यों का वृत्तांत’ आकार लेता है। उसकी सँभली पंक्तियों से आगे की पीढ़ियाँ गुप्त भारत को झाँकती हैं: उसके अस्पताल और राज–मार्ग, उसके स्वर्णिम दरबार और नि:शब्द श्रमिक, उसकी रोशनी और उसका अँधेरा।

panels.shot: slow pull-back from a modern scholar’s desk stacked with books, with Faxian’s figure imagined faintly in the background
panels.action: On a modern scholar’s desk lie open volumes labeled with Faxian’s name, alongside works by R.S. Sharma, Romila Thapar, and Upinder Singh, while a faint, ghostly image of Faxian looks on from the background.

dialogue.text: Today, historians place his gentle praise beside hard evidence, seeing the Gupta age as bright yet uneven. Through Faxian’s foreign eyes, we weigh a kingdom not only by its gold, but by its grace to the least.

i18n.hi: आज इतिहासकार उसकी सौम्य प्रशंसा को कठोर सबूतों के साथ रखते हैं, गुप्त युग को तेज पर असमान मानते हुए। फाशियान की विदेशी आँखों के सहारे हम राज्य को केवल सोने से नहीं, सबसे छोटे पर उसकी कृपा से भी तौलते हैं।

title_i18n.en: Faxian's Road Through a Golden Land
title_i18n.hi: स्वर्ण भूमि में फाशियान की राह

subtitle_i18n.en: A foreign monk walks the shining roads of Gupta India and sees both gold and shadow
subtitle_i18n.hi: एक विदेशी भिक्षु गुप्त भारत की चमकती राहों पर चलता है, जहाँ सोना भी है, साया भी
